mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL values indicate accelerating inflation expectations, which forces equity valuations lower as discount rates rise, generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and signals economic deceleration, causing S&P 500 prices to decline over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or mean return difference is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in GDPC1 and HOUST signals robust aggregate demand and housing market strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in INDPRO indicates immediate supply chain bottlenecks and production slowdowns, triggering short-term risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered observations or the signal loses predictive power during stable growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent, higher borrowing costs compress equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, resulting in negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of high-rate observations or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in PAYEMS reflect a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no significant correlation with employment growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising RSAFS and falling HOUST indicates shifting consumer demand toward financial assets away from real estate, creating short-term liquidity inflows that push S&P 500 prices higher within five days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS mom change > 0.2% and HOUST mom change < -1.0%
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: High INDPRO readings during a supply-constrained regime signal capacity utilization limits that eventually force production cuts, leading to negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-INDPRO observations during supply bottlenecks or the regime filter adds no incremental predictive value.
